workPackage.title: Extract convoluted workflow rule groups into dedicated skill+resource sets

steps.summary: Defined problem: workflow TOON files embed ~140+ behavioral rules as prose text with heavy duplication across 10 workflows. Three rule layers identified: global (85 rules in meta/rules.toon), workflow-level (118 rules across 10 workflow.toon files), skill-level (already formalized).

Classified as Inventive Goal — Improvement with Complex complexity. Not broken but architecturally suboptimal. Touches all 3 rule layers, requires server code changes, and affects behavioral contracts.
Selected research-only path. Requirements are clear from prior analysis; elicitation unnecessary. Research needed for skill/resource structure patterns and start_session slimming approach.

Created design philosophy with problem statement, system context (3-layer rule architecture), impact assessment, classification rationale, workflow path decision, constraints, success criteria, and initial design decisions.
Identified 9 assumptions across Problem Interpretation, Complexity Assessment, and Workflow Path categories.
Created assumptions log with 9 assumptions. Reconciliation converged in 1 iteration: 5 validated through code analysis, 4 open (non-code-resolvable).

Reconciliation loop converged. Validated: list_workflows has no meta filter, start_session returns full 85-rule payload, meta/skills/ provides universal fallback, RulesSchema supports minimal content, tests exist that need updating. Open: external consumer impact, priority ordering, context efficiency, hybrid rule model.

checkpoints.summary: Inventive Goal — Improvement, Complex complexity confirmed
Research only path selected — skip elicitation, research skill/resource patterns

codebaseEvidence: listWorkflows() in workflow-loader.ts:119-157 has no meta exclusion — iterates all directories with workflow.toon
start_session in resource-tools.ts:40-79 calls readRules() which returns full meta/rules.toon content unconditionally
readSkill() in skill-loader.ts:101-134 uses layered search with meta/skills/ as universal fallback
RulesSchema in rules.schema.ts:10-17 allows empty rules arrays via optional() — minimal file is valid
rules-loader.test.ts and mcp-server.test.ts both assert on current rules structure

transition.rationale: Requirements clear, research needed for skill structure patterns
